{{基礎情報 公家
| 氏名 = Ujihiko
| 画像 =
| 画像サイズ =
| 画像説明 =
| 時代 = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}
| 生誕 = Unknown
| 死没 = Unknown
| 改名 =
| 別名 =
| 諡号 =
| 神号 =
| 戒名 =
| 墓所 =
| 官位 = 6th {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}}
| 主君 =
| 氏族 = Ancestor of the {{ill|Ki clan (shinbetsu)|ja|紀氏 (神別)|lt=Ki-no-Kimi|lt_ja=紀君}}
| 父母 = Father: {{ill|Ōnakusahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大名草彦命|lt=Ōnakusa-hiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=大名草比古命}}<ref> Suzuki Masanobu, "The Formation and Background of the Kii-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Shidai" [https://123deta.com/document/ydvxje1y-紀伊国造次第の成立とその背景.html]</ref>
| 兄弟 = {{ill|Yamashita-kage-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|山下影比売命}}<ref>''Kojiki''</ref>, one theory: Kimi-tsumi-no-Mikoto<ref>"Izumi Shinbetsu Tenjin Ōmura-no-Atai," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku''.</ref>
| 妻   =
| 子   = {{ill|Funemoto-no-Mikoto|ja|舟本命}}, Kagehime<ref>''Nihon Shoki''</ref>
| 特記事項 =
}}
'''Ujihiko''' (''ujihiko''; ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''), also '''Uzuhiko''' (''uzuhiko''; ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''), was a {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|gōzoku|ja|豪族}} appearing in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀}} and one of the '''{{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}}'''. Through the descendants of his son-in-law {{ill|Takenouchi-no-Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, he also became the ancestor of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}}<ref name="講談社人名大辞典-菟道彦"/>{{refnest|group="注"|There is a theory that {{ill|Takenouchi-no-Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} was a fictional figure, constructed from episodes centering on the {{ill|Soga clan|ja|蘇我氏}} and {{ill|Nakatomi no Kamatari|ja|中臣鎌足}} after the formation of the ''{{ill|Kyūji|ja|旧辞}}''<ref name="広辞典-宿禰"/>.}}.

==Overview==
A {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, and regarded as the founding ancestor of the {{ill|shinbetsu|ja|神別}} {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko house|ja|紀伊国造|lt=Kii-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko house|lt_ja=紀伊国造家}}<ref name="広辞典-紀氏"/><ref name="ニッポニカ-紀氏"/>. His {{ill|uji|ja|氏#古代氏族としての「氏」|lt=uji|lt_ja=氏}} is {{ill|Ki (shinbetsu)|ja|紀氏 (神別)|lt=Ki|lt_ja=紀}}, and his {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} is {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}} (''Nihon Shoki'')<ref>National Diet Library Digital Collections, Kokushi Taikei Vol. 1. ''Nihon Shoki''. {{NDLJP|991091}} Vol. 7, frame 74.</ref>.

In the ''Nihon Shoki'', he married his daughter {{ill|Kagehime|ja|影媛}} to {{ill|Yanushi-oshio-taketoshin-no-Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命}}, who had come down to {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}} as {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s proxy<ref name="講談社人名大辞典-影媛1"/><ref name="講談社人名大辞典-屋主忍男武雄心命"/>. Kagehime gave birth to {{ill|Takenouchi-no-Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, and the descendants of his son {{ill|Ki-no-Tsunu-no-Sukune|ja|木角宿禰}} became the {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏}}<ref name="国史大辞典-紀氏"/>. On the other hand, Ujihiko's direct line became the shinbetsu Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko house<ref name="広辞典-紀氏"/><ref name="ニッポニカ-紀氏"/>. Yanushi-oshio-taketoshin-no-Mikoto is said to have stayed in Kii Province for 9 years<ref name="講談社人名大辞典-屋主忍男武雄心命"/>; his residence was "{{読み仮名|Abi-no-Kashiwabara|abi no kashiwabara}}"<ref name="講談社人名大辞典-菟道彦"/>. In the ''{{ill|Kii Zoku-Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}'' compiled in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|lt=Edo period|lt_ja=江戸時代}}, "Abi-no-Kashiwabara" is identified with present-day {{ill|Matsubara (Wakayama)|ja|松原村 (和歌山県)|lt=Matsubara|lt_ja=松原}} in {{ill|Wakayama, Wakayama|ja|和歌山市|lt=Wakayama City|lt_ja=和歌山市}}, where there is a "well where Takenouchi-no-Sukune was born"<ref name="平凡地名-柏原"/>.

In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' he is written as {{読み仮名|Uzuhiko|uzuhiko}}, and his younger sister {{ill|Yamashita-kage-hime|ja|山下影日売}} is recorded as having given birth to {{ill|Takenouchi-no-Sukune|ja|建内宿禰}} with {{ill|Hiko-futsu-oshi-no-Makoto-no-Mikoto|ja|比古布都押之信命}}<ref name="講談社人名大辞典-影媛1"/><ref name="講談社人名大辞典-菟道彦"/>.

A theory holds that this series of records in the ''Kiki'' is based on the family records of the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|lt=Ki clan|lt_ja=紀一族}}, and ancient Japanese historian {{ill|Kishi Toshio|ja|岸俊男}} (1920–1987, professor emeritus of {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}) noted that careful verification is required to determine whether these are historical facts<ref name="国史大辞典-紀氏"/>.

== Genealogy ==
His father was Ōnakusa-hiko-no-Mikoto, 4th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amichine-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}}; his younger sister {{ill|Yamashita-kage-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|山下影比売命}} became the wife of {{ill|Yanushi-oshio-taketoshin-no-Mikoto|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命}} and bore {{ill|Takenouchi-no-Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''</ref>.

He had two children: Funemoto-no-Mikoto, ancestor of the Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and a daughter — wife of Takenouchi-no-Sukune and mother of {{ill|Ki-no-Tsunu-no-Sukune|ja|紀角宿禰}}. In the "Family Register of Niu in Amano-no-Shō, Ito District," Ujihiko's son appears to be read as Toyomimi-no-Mikoto (Toyomimi-no-Mikoto). The ''Kii Zoku-Fudoki'' puts forth the theory that the two generations of Funemoto-no-Mikoto and Yatsukashi-hiko-no-Mikoto, placed between Ujihiko and Toyomimi-no-Mikoto, should be interpreted as "Niu (Funemoto) Ya (Ya) Tsugi-no-shi (Tsukashi)" — an epithet attached to Toyomimi-no-Mikoto.

==Footnotes==
===Notes===
<references group="注"/>
===Citations===
{{Reflist|colwidth=30em
|refs=
*<ref name="講談社人名大辞典-影媛1">''Nihon Jinmei Daijiten'', {{ill|Kōdansha|ja|講談社}}, {{ill|Japan Knowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ|lt=Japan Knowledge|lt_ja=ジャパンナレッジ}} edition. "Kagehime (1)"</ref>

*<ref name="講談社人名大辞典-菟道彦">''Nihon Jinmei Daijiten'', {{ill|Kōdansha|ja|講談社}}, Japan Knowledge edition. "Ujihiko"</ref>

*<ref name="講談社人名大辞典-屋主忍男武雄心命">''Nihon Jinmei Daijiten'', {{ill|Kōdansha|ja|講談社}}, Japan Knowledge edition. "Yanushi-oshio-taketoshin-no-Mikoto"</ref>

*<ref name="国史大辞典-紀氏">''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|lt=Kokushi Daijiten|lt_ja=国史大辞典}}'', 1979–1997, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, Japan Knowledge edition. {{ill|Kishi Toshio|ja|岸俊男}}, "Ki-shi"</ref>

*<ref name="ニッポニカ-紀氏">''{{ill|Nihon Dai Hyakka Zensho|ja|日本大百科全書}}'', {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, Japan Knowledge edition. {{ill|Mayuzumi Hiromichi|ja|黛弘道}}, "Ki-shi"</ref>

*<ref name="広辞典-紀氏">''Nihon-shi Kō-jiten'', p. 559, "Ki-shi"</ref>
*<ref name="広辞典-宿禰">''Nihon-shi Kō-jiten'', p. 1343, "Takenouchi-no-Sukune"</ref>

*<ref name="平凡地名-柏原">''{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}'' (Vol. 31 Wakayama Prefecture Place Names), {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, Japan Knowledge edition. "Kashiwabara"</ref>
<!--
<ref name="houga">[[宝賀寿男]]「第1章 天神系氏族　第6節 紀国造氏族　1紀国造紀直、紀宿祢（一）」『古代氏族系譜集成』中巻、古代氏族研究会、879-881頁。</ref>
-->
}}

===Bibliography===
*''Nihon-shi Kō-jiten'', Nihon-shi Kō-jiten Editorial Committee (eds.), {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}, 1997 (1st ed., 1st print). ISBN 4-634-62010-3

== See also ==
*{{ill|List of Figures from before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}
*{{ill|Kitone-no-Mikoto|ja|鬼刀禰命}}
*{{ill|Chinaso-no-Mikoto|ja|智名曽命}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:うしひこ}}
[[Category:日本神話の人物]]
[[Category:古墳時代の人物]]
[[Category:古代日本の人物]]
[[Category:和歌山県の歴史]]
[[Category:紀伊国の人物]]
[[Category:生没年不詳]]
[[Category:紀氏]]

{{Shinto-stub}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:莵道彦]]
